requirements.full_sentence:
Remittances for Import Payments
AD Category I Banks may allow remittance for making payments for imports into
India, after ensuring that all the requisite details are made available by the importer
and the remittance is for bona fide trade transactions as per applicable laws in
force.
requirements.object: payments for imports into India, after ensuring that all the requisite details are made available by the importer and the

requirements.full_sentence:
Import Licences
Except for goods included in the negative list which require licence under the
Foreign Trade Policy in force, AD Category - I banks may freely open letters of
credit and allow remittances for import.
requirements.object: credit and allow remittances for import

requirements.full_sentence:
While opening letters of credit, the ‘For
Exchange Control purposes’ copy of the licence should be called for and adherence
to special conditions, if any, attached to such licences should be ensured.
requirements.object: adherence to special conditions

requirements.full_sentence:
After
effecting remittances under the licence, AD Category - I banks may preserve the
copies of utilised licence /s till they are verified by the internal auditors or inspectors.
requirements.object: utilised licence /s till they are verified by the internal auditors or inspectors

2020, the time period for completion of remittances against normal imports (except
in cases where amounts are withheld towards guarantee of performance etc.) was
extended from six months to twelve months from the date of shipment for such
imports made on or before July 31, 2020.

requirements.full_sentence:
Time Limit for Deferred Payment Arrangements
Any deferred payment arrangements (including suppliers’ and buyers’ credit) entered
into, for up to three years in case of import of capital goods and up to one year or the
operating cycle whichever is less, in case of import of non-capital goods, shall be
treated as trade credits for which the procedural guidelines as laid down in the Master
Direction on External Commercial Borrowings, Trade Credits and Structured
Obligations may be followed.7

Time Limit for Import of Books
Remittances against import of books may be allowed without restriction as to the
time limit, provided, interest payment, if any, is as per the instructions in para C.2 of
Section III of this Circular.

Advance Remittance for the Import of Services
AD Category – I bank may allow advance remittance for import of services without

conditions: Where the amount of advance exceeds USD 500

if such a guarantee is issued against the
counter-guarantee of a bank of international repute situated outside India
requirements.full_sentence:
(a) Where the amount of advance exceeds USD 500,000 or its equivalent, a
guarantee from a bank of international repute situated outside India, or a guarantee
from an AD Category – I bank in India, if such a guarantee is issued against the
counter-guarantee of a bank of international repute situated outside India, should be
obtained from the overseas beneficiary.
requirements.object: overseas beneficiary

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) AD Category – I banks should also follow-up to ensure that the beneficiary of the
advance remittance fulfils his obligation under the contract or agreement with the
remitter in India, failing which, the amount should be repatriated to India.

conditions: Where goods are short-supplied
requirements.full_sentence:
Remittances against Replacement Imports
Where goods are short-supplied, damaged, short-landed or lost in transit and the
Exchange Control Copy of the import licence has already been utilised to cover the
opening of a letter of credit against the original goods which have been lost, the
original endorsement to the extent of the value of the lost goods may be cancelled
by the AD Category – I bank and fresh remittance for replacement imports may be
permitted without reference to Reserve Bank, provided, the insurance claim relating
to the lost goods has been settled in favour of the importer.
requirements.object: AD Category – I bank and fresh remittance for replacement imports may be permitted without reference to Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
It may be ensured that
the consignment being replaced is shipped within the validity period of the license.
requirements.object: consignment being replaced is shipped within the validity period of the license

requirements.full_sentence:
20AD bank should ensure that proper remark/indicator is entered for ORM mark
off/closure of Bills in IDPMS etc.
requirements.object: is entered for ORM mark off/closure of Bills in IDPMS etc

requirements.full_sentence:
Guarantee for Replacement Import
In case replacement goods for defective import are being sent by the overseas
supplier before the defective goods imported earlier are reshipped out of India, AD
Category-I banks may issue guarantees at the request of importer client for
dispatch/return of the defective goods, according to their commercial judgment.
requirements.object: request of importer client for dispatch/return of the defective goods, according to their commercial judgment

requirements.full_sentence:
Import of Equipment by Business Process Outsourcing (BPO) Companies for
their Overseas Sites
AD Category – I bank may allow BPO companies in India to make remittances
towards the cost of equipment to be imported and installed at their overseas sites in
connection with the setting up of their International Call Centres (ICCs) subject to
the following conditions:

to which extension is granted will be indicated in “Extension Date” column.

requirements.full_sentence:
Suppliers’ and Buyers’ credit (trade credit) including the usance period of Letters of
Credit opened for import of gold in any form, including jewellery made of
gold/precious metals or/and studded with diamonds/semi- precious/precious stones,
should not exceed 90 days from the date of shipment.
requirements.object: the date of shipment

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks may allow Qualified Jewellers to remit advance payments for
eleven days for import of gold through IIBX in compliance to the extant
Foreign Trade Policy and regulations issued under IFSC Act.
requirements.object: remit advance payments for eleven days for import of gold through IIBX in compliance to the extant Foreign Trade Policy a

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks
shall ensure that advance remittance for such import through
exchange/s authorised by IFSCA shall be as per the terms of the sale
contract or other document in the nature of an irrevocable purchase
order in terms of IFSC Act and regulations made thereunder by IFSCA.
requirements.object: for such import through exchange/s authorised by IFSCA shall be as per the terms of the sale contract or other document

requirements.full_sentence:
AD bank shall carry out all the due diligence and ensure the
remittances sent are only for the bona fide import transactions through
exchange/s authorised by IFSCA.
requirements.object: due diligence and ensure the remittances sent are only for the bona fide import transactions through exchange/s authorised by IFSCA

requirements.full_sentence:
The advance remittance for import of Gold should not be leveraged in
what-so-ever form for importing Gold worth more than the advance
remittance made.
requirements.object: form for importing Gold worth more than the advance remittance made

conditions: In case the import of Gold through IFSCA authorised exchange
requirements.full_sentence:
In case the import of Gold through IFSCA authorised exchange, for
which advance remittance has been made, does not materialise, or the
advance remittance made for the purpose is more than the amount
required, the unutilized advance remittance shall be remitted back to
the same AD bank within the specified time limit of eleven days.
requirements.object: the same AD bank within the specified time limit of eleven days

requirements.full_sentence:
For gold imported through IIBX, QJ shall submit the Bill of Entry (or any
other such applicable document issued/approved by Customs
Department for evidence of import), issued by Customs Authorities to
the AD bank from where advance payment has been remitted.
requirements.object: Entry (or any other such applicable document issued/approved by Customs Department for evidence of import), issued by Customs Autho

requirements.full_sentence:
All payments by Qualified Jewellers for imports of gold through IIBX,
shall be made through exchange mechanism as approved by IFSCA in
terms of IFSC Act and regulations.
requirements.object: mechanism as approved by IFSCA in terms of IFSC Act and regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
IFSCA shall also put in place necessary system to
ensure that the advance remittance received from QJs are solely for the purpose
for import of gold through IIBX.
requirements.object: necessary system to ensure that the advance remittance received from QJs are solely for the purpose for import of gold through IIBX

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks may frame their own internal guidelines to deal with such cases, with
the approval of their Board of Directors.
requirements.object: guidelines to deal with such cases, with the approval of their Board of Directors

requirements.full_sentence:
AD bank shall create Outward Remittance Message (ORM) for all such
outward remittances in IDPMS in terms of extant guidelines.
requirements.object: (ORM) for all such outward remittances in IDPMS in terms of extant guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
AD bank shall report the import of gold through QJ in CIMS as
prescribed at para C.11.1 above.
requirements.object: gold through QJ in CIMS as prescribed at para C

notified by –The International Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA)

subject to the directions as mentioned in A.P.

conditions such as: (i) AD banks being satisfied of the genuineness of the reason

the additional period;

respective half year 45

September and October-March) of such extensions allowed customer-wise, to the

requirements.full_sentence:
Import of Platinum / Silver on Unfixed Price Basis
The nominated agency/bank may allow import of platinum and silver, on outright
purchase basis subject to the condition that although ownership of the same shall
be passed on to the importers at the time of import itself, the price shall be fixed
later as and when the importer sells to the user.
requirements.object: the importers at the time of import itself, the price shall be fixed later as and when the importer sells to the user

requirements.full_sentence:
C.12.3 Import of Silver by Qualified Jewellers as notified by –The International
Financial Services Centres Authority (IFSCA) 46
AD Category-I banks may allow Qualified Jewellers to remit advance payment for
eleven days for import of silver through IIBX subject to the conditions as mentioned in
A.P.
requirements.object: remit advance payment for eleven days for import of silver through IIBX subject to the conditions as mentioned in A

requirements.full_sentence: (DIR Series) Circular No.04 dated May 25, 2022.

(i) AD Category – I bank may enter into arrangements with international factoring
companies of repute, preferably members of Factors Chain International, without
the approval of Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: international factoring companies of repute, preferably members of Factors Chain International, without the approval of R

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks may handle the Merchanting Trade Transactions (MTT) subject 
to the following guidelines:
i.
requirements.object: Transactions (MTT) subject to the following guidelines: i

requirements.full_sentence:
For a trade to be classified as merchanting trade, goods acquired shall not 
enter the Domestic Tariff Area.
requirements.object: Area

requirements.full_sentence:
Considering that in some cases, the goods acquired may require certain 
specific processing/ value-addition, the state of goods so acquired may be 
allowed transformation subject to the AD bank being satisfied with the 
documentary evidence and bonafides of the transaction.
requirements.object: value-addition, the state of goods so acquired may be allowed transformation subject to the AD bank being satis

requirements.full_sentence:
The MTT shall be undertaken for the goods that are permitted for exports / 
imports under the prevailing Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) of India as on the 
date of shipment.
requirements.object: goods that are permitted for exports / imports under the prevailing Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) of India as on the date of ship

requirements.full_sentence:
All rules, regulations and directions applicable to exports 
(except Export Declaration Form) and imports (except Bill of Entry) shall be 
complied with for the export leg and import leg respectively.
requirements.object: the export leg and import leg respectively

requirements.full_sentence: AD bank shall satisfy itself with the bonafides of the transactions.
requirements.object: bonafides of the transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, 
KYC and AML guidelines shall be scrupulously adhered to by the AD bank 
while handling such transactions.
requirements.object: by the AD bank while handling such transactions

conditions: if originals are not available
requirements.full_sentence:
The 
AD bank shall verify the documents like invoice, packing list, transport 
documents and insurance documents (if originals are not available, Non- 
negotiable copies duly authenticated by the bank handling documents may 
be taken) and satisfy itself about the genuineness of the trade.
requirements.object: invoice, packing list, transport documents and insurance documents (if originals are not available, Non- negotiable copies

requirements.full_sentence:
However, the AD bank shall ensure that the requisite details are made 
available /retrievable at the time of Inspection/Audit/investigation of the 
transactions.
requirements.object: details are made available /retrievable at the time of Inspection/Audit/investigation of the transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
The entire MTT shall be completed within an overall period of nine months 
and there shall not be any outlay of foreign exchange beyond six months48.
requirements.object: foreign exchange beyond six months48

requirements.full_sentence:
The commencement date of merchanting trade shall be the date of shipment 
/ export leg receipt or import leg payment, whichever is first.
requirements.object: shipment / export leg receipt or import leg payment, whichever is first

requirements.full_sentence:
The completion 
date shall be the date of shipment / export leg receipt or import leg payment, 
whichever is the last.
requirements.object: shipment / export leg receipt or import leg payment, whichever is the last

requirements.full_sentence:
Short-term credit either by way of suppliers' credit or buyers' credit may be 
extended for MTT to the extent not backed by advance remittance for the 
export leg, including the discounting of export leg LC by the AD bank, as in 
the case of import transactions.
requirements.object: to the extent not backed by advance remittance for the export leg, including the discounting of export leg LC by the AD bank, as

requirements.full_sentence:
However, Letter of Undertaking (LoU)/ Letter 
of Comfort (LoC) shall not be issued for supplier’s/ buyer’s credit.
requirements.object: buyer’s credit

requirements.full_sentence:
Any receipts for the export leg, prior to the payment for import leg, may be 
parked either in Exchange Earners Foreign Currency (EEFC) account or in 
an interest-bearing INR account till the import leg liability arises.
requirements.object: Exchange Earners Foreign Currency (EEFC) account or in an interest-bearing INR account till the import leg liability arises

requirements.full_sentence:
It shall be 
strictly earmarked/ lien-marked for the payment of import leg and the liability 
of the import leg, as soon as it arises, shall be extinguished out of these 
funds without any delay.
requirements.object: for the payment of import leg and the liability of the import leg, as soon as it arises, shall be extinguished ou

conditions:
If such receipts are kept in interest-bearing INR 
account
requirements.full_sentence:
If such receipts are kept in interest-bearing INR 
account, hedging thereof may be allowed by the AD bank at the request of its 
customer, as per extant regulations.
requirements.object: AD bank at the request of its customer, as per extant regulations

requirements.full_sentence:
No fund/non-fund-based facilities shall 
be extended against these balances.
requirements.object: balances

conditions:
In case of discounting of export leg LC where payment for import leg is still to 
be made (even if partially)
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of discounting of export leg LC where payment for import leg is still to 
be made (even if partially), the proceeds shall be utilized in the manner 
prescribed at point no.
requirements.object: manner prescribed at point no

requirements.full_sentence:
Payment for import leg may also be allowed to be made out of the balances
48 Inserted vide A.P.
requirements.object: be made out of the balances 48 Inserted vide A

requirements.full_sentence:
Merchanting traders may be allowed to make advance payment for the 
import leg on demand made by the overseas supplier.
requirements.object: advance payment for the import leg on demand made by the overseas supplier

conditions:
In case where inward 
remittance from the overseas buyer is not received before the outward 
remittance to the overseas supplier
requirements.full_sentence:
In case where inward 
remittance from the overseas buyer is not received before the outward 
remittance to the overseas supplier, AD bank may handle such transactions 
based on its commercial judgement.
requirements.object: on its commercial judgement

requirements.full_sentence:
It may, however, be ensured that any 
such advance payment for an import leg beyond USD 500,000/- per 
transaction, shall be made against Bank Guarantee / an unconditional, 
irrevocable standby Letter of Credit from an international bank of repute.
requirements.object: Guarantee / an unconditional, irrevocable standby Letter of Credit from an international bank of repute

requirements.full_sentence:
Overall prudential limits on allowing such advance payments by a customer 
may be fixed by the AD bank.
requirements.object: AD bank

conditions:
in case of each MTT and report 
defaults in any leg by the traders to the concerned Regional Office of the 
Reserve Bank
requirements.timeline:
within 15 days 
from the close of each half year
requirements.full_sentence:
AD bank shall ensure one-to-one matching in case of each MTT and report 
defaults in any leg by the traders to the concerned Regional Office of the 
Reserve Bank, on half yearly basis in the format as annexed, within 15 days 
from the close of each half year, i.e.
requirements.object: case of each MTT and report defaults in any leg by the traders to the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank, o

requirements.full_sentence:
Merchant traders with outstanding of 5% or more of their annual export 
earnings shall be liable for caution listing.
requirements.object: listing

requirements.full_sentence:
C.14.2 The merchanting traders shall be genuine traders of goods and not mere 
financial intermediaries.
requirements.object: goods and not mere financial intermediaries

requirements.full_sentence:
Confirmed orders must be received by them from the 
overseas buyers.
requirements.object: from the overseas buyers

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks shall satisfy themselves about the capabilities of the 
merchanting trader to perform the obligations under the order.
requirements.object: capabilities of the merchanting trader to perform the obligations under the order

requirements.full_sentence:
The merchanting 
trade shall result in profit which shall be determined by subtracting import 
payments and related expenses from export proceeds for the specific MTT.
requirements.object: shall be determined by subtracting import payments and related expenses from export proceeds for the specific MTT

requirements.full_sentence:
AD bank may write-off the unrealized amount of export leg, without any 
ceiling, on the request made by the Merchanting trader, in the following 
circumstances: 
a.
requirements.object: of export leg, without any ceiling, on the request made by the Merchanting trader, in the following circumstances:

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to above, write-off as at (i) shall be subject to following 
conditions: 
a.
requirements.object: conditions: a

requirements.full_sentence:
AD bank shall satisfy itself with the bonafides of the transactions and ensure
that there are no KYC/AML concerns.
requirements.object: bonafides of the transactions and ensure that there are no KYC/AML concerns

requirements.full_sentence:
The transaction shall not be under investigation under FEMA by any of the
investigating agency/ies.
requirements.object: FEMA by any of the investigating agency/ies

requirements.full_sentence:
However, AD banks may allow 
payment of agency commission up to a reasonable extent by way of outward 
remittance under exceptional circumstances, subject to the following conditions:
a.
requirements.object: commission up to a reasonable extent by way of outward remittance under exceptional circumstances

requirements.full_sentence:
The payment of agency commission shall not result in the MTT ending into
a loss.
requirements.object: ending into a loss

requirements.full_sentence:
The Merchanting trader shall make a specific request to the AD bank in 
this regard.
requirements.object: to the AD bank in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
C.14.6 AD bank may approach Regional Office (RO) concerned of the Reserve 
Bank for regularization of the MTT for deviation, if any, from the prescribed 
guidelines and the MTT shall be closed only after receiving approval from the RO 
concerned of the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: receiving approval from the RO concerned of the Reserve Bank

conditions:
where the invoicing is in a freely convertible currency and the settlement takes
place in the currency of the beneficiary
requirements.full_sentence:
Settlement of Import transactions in currencies not having a direct exchange
rate
To further liberalize the procedure and facilitate settlement of import transactions
where the invoicing is in a freely convertible currency and the settlement takes
place in the currency of the beneficiary, which though convertible, does not have a
direct exchange rate, it has been decided that AD Category-I banks may permit
settlement of such import transactions (excluding those put through the ACU
mechanism), subject to conditions as under:
requirements.object: import transactions (excluding those put through the ACU mechanism)

requirements.full_sentence: (a) Importer shall be a customer of the AD Bank,
requirements.object: the AD Bank,

requirements.full_sentence:
49 Inserted vide AP (DIR) Series Circular 42 dated February 4, 2016
34
50C.17 Import Data Processing and Monitoring System (IDPMS) – reconciliation of 
import entries – Special Procedure
Notwithstanding anything contained in this master direction, AD banks shall adopt the 
following procedure while closing entries (including outstanding entries) in IDPMS of 
value equivalent to ₹10 lakh per entry/bill or less:
a.
requirements.object: while closing entries (including outstanding entries) in IDPMS of value equivalent to ₹10 lakh per entry/bill or less:

requirements.full_sentence:
Such entries shall be reconciled and closed based on a declaration provided by the 
concerned importer that the amount has been paid.
requirements.object: based on a declaration provided by the concerned importer that the amount has been paid

requirements.full_sentence:
Any reduction in declared value or invoice value of the bills of entry shall also be 
accepted, based on the declaration by the concerned importer.
requirements.object: on the declaration by the concerned importer

requirements.full_sentence:
The declarations referred above may also be received on a quarterly basis from the 
importers in a consolidated manner (by combining several bills in one declaration) for 
bulk reconciliation and closing of IDPMS entries.
requirements.object: a quarterly basis from the importers in a consolidated manner (by combining several bills in one declaration) for bulk reconcili

requirements.full_sentence:
Accordlingly, AD banks shall also review the charges levied for handling these 
small-value import transactions, keeping in view the revised procedure/relaxations 
mentioned above and ensure that the same are commensurate with the services 
rendered.
requirements.object: levied for handling these small-value import transactions, keeping in view the revised procedure/relaxations mentioned above

requirements.full_sentence:
AD banks shall not levy any penal charges (penalty) for delays in adherence 
to any regulatory guidelines.
requirements.object: (penalty) for delays in adherence to any regulatory guidelines
